originalTitle: Desperate for food drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
title: Desperate for food drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
content: Some 26 million people are "facing extreme hunger" in Kenya, Ethiopia and Somalia, warns humanitarian organisation Oxfam.

originalTitle: UK agrees deal to ease migrant returns to Nigeria
title: Friends Across the Ocean! Good News for Nigeria and the UK!
content:
Hello Amazing Friends!

Hello everyone! Are you ready for some really exciting news from far, far away? Imagine two big countries, Nigeria and the United Kingdom, becoming even better friends! It’s like when you make a new friend at school, and you decide to play together and help each other. Well, that’s kind of what’s happening, but with whole countries!

Nigeria is a super big country in Africa, with lots of people, beautiful places, and amazing stories. The United Kingdom, or the UK, is a country far away across the sea. It has famous castles, kings, and queens! And guess what? These two countries are making a special promise to help each other even more.

What is a "Deal"?

You might hear grown-ups talk about a "deal." What does that mean? It's like when you and your friend decide, "Okay, you can play with my red car, and I'll play with your blue car, and we'll share!" It's an agreement, a promise to do something together. So, Nigeria and the UK have made a "deal" to work together on something important.

Did you know? Nigeria is the most populated country in Africa! That means lots and lots of people live there. It's truly a vibrant place!

Helping People Go Home Safely

Sometimes, people travel from one country to another. They might go to visit, or to work, or to learn new things. These people are sometimes called "migrants." And sometimes, after a while, they want to go back to their own country, to their families and homes. This "deal" is about making it easier and safer for people who want to go back home to Nigeria from the UK.

Imagine you went to a friend's house to play, and then it's time to go home. You want a safe and easy way to get back to your own bed, right? This deal is about making sure people can do that safely and with help, making their journey home much smoother.

A Special Visitor from Nigeria

This big news happened because a very important person from Nigeria went on a special trip. His name is President Bola Tinubu. He is like the head teacher or the captain of the whole country of Nigeria! He helps make big decisions and makes sure everyone is doing well.

President Tinubu travelled all the way from Nigeria to the United Kingdom. It’s a very long journey, usually by airplane! He went to meet other important leaders there, like the Prime Minister of the UK. When important leaders visit other countries, it's a very big deal!

Did you know? The President of a country is chosen by the people! They help lead the country, just like your parents help lead your family.

What is a "State Visit"?

President Tinubu’s trip wasn't just any visit; it was a "state visit." A state visit is super special! It's like when a king or queen or a very important leader visits another country officially. There are lots of polite meetings, special dinners, and they talk about important things to make their countries better friends.

It's a way for countries to show respect for each other and talk about big plans. Think of it like a very fancy birthday party where important grown-ups talk about how to make the world a better place!

Did you know? The UK has a Queen (Queen Camilla) and a King (King Charles III)! They live in famous palaces like Buckingham Palace.

A Very Long Time Ago!

Guess what made this visit even more special? It was the *first* time a leader from West Africa had a state visit to the UK in a super long time – 37 years! Wow, that's even older than your parents or grandparents in some cases! Imagine not seeing a close friend for 37 years and then finally getting to visit and talk about everything. That’s how important this visit was!

West Africa is a part of Africa where Nigeria is located. It has many wonderful countries all close together, sharing beautiful cultures and friendly people. So, President Tinubu's visit was a big moment for all of West Africa too!

Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with 54 different countries! West Africa is just one part of this amazing continent.

Why is This Deal Good?

So, why is this deal about helping people go home safely so good? Well, it means that if someone from Nigeria is in the UK and wants to go back to their family, they will get help to do it properly. It's about being kind and organised. Both countries want to make sure people are safe and happy.

It also means that Nigeria and the UK are talking more and working together. When countries are friends and help each other, they can learn so much! They can share ideas about schools, hospitals, how to grow food, and lots of other things that make people's lives better.

This helps everyone! It helps the people who are returning home feel safe and supported. It helps both countries build stronger friendships and work on other important things together, like trade, which means selling and buying things from each other, like delicious Nigerian foods or cool UK toys!

When leaders visit each other and make agreements, it’s a sign that they care about making things better for everyone. It shows they want peace and good relationships. It’s a bit like when you and your friends promise to always be kind to each other – it makes playtime much more fun and fair!

Friends Forever!

So, the big news is that Nigeria and the UK are making their friendship even stronger. President Tinubu's special trip has brought these two countries closer, and they are working together to help people and make things better for the future. It's a wonderful example of how countries can be friends, help each other, and make the world a happier place for everyone!

Hooray for Nigeria! Hooray for the UK! And hooray for friendship!
summary: Nigeria and the United Kingdom have made a special agreement to help people who want to return home to Nigeria, showing how two countries can be good friends and work together.
whyMatters: This is important because it means countries are helping people travel safely, and it shows that Nigeria and the UK are building stronger friendships to help each other grow and learn, just like good friends do.

originalTitle: Tailgating at matches to become criminal offence
title: Tailgating at matches to become criminal offence
content: Entering a football match in England and Wales without a ticket will become a criminal offence under new laws.
